exemplar.title: M25 #2: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #2: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a sustained focus on the prescribed title, consistently returning to the contrast between fragility and robustness of knowledge
Effectively links discussion to two areas of knowledge (arts/dance and natural sciences/physics) using clear examples (ballet, capoeira, Newtonian mechanics, quantum mechanics)
Demonstrates a basic claimâcounterclaim structure and shows awareness of alternative perspectives (e.g., adaptability of dance vs. paradigm shifts in physics)
Selects illustrative examples (waveâparticle duality, bans on Native-American dances) that highlight the dynamics of knowledge change
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive, with limited examination of how specific ways of knowing or knowledge-production methods underpin the arguments
Examples are sometimes generic or mismatch the promised scope (mathematics was outlined but not developed), and several references function as unsupported name-drops
Argument evaluation is asserted rather than rigorously argued; counterclaims lack explicit links back to the central knowledge question
No formal knowledge question is posed to guide the structure, and the essayâs academic tone and citation rigor are inconsistent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Given the official total of 8, the essayâs clear focus on the title and use of relevant AOK examples justify a strong mark. However, because the analysis is still largely descriptive and lacks the depth, rigour of evaluation, and fully integrated TOK concepts required for the highest bands, the essay does not merit full marks. Assigning 8 reflects its solid engagement and structure while acknowledging the need for deeper analytical development and more coherent TOK framing.

annotations.comment: The student clearly restates and focuses on the prescribed title, maintaining a consistent link to the notion of fragile versus robust knowledge across areas of knowledge.

The introduction promises exploration of mathematics but proceeds with physics examples instead; the chosen second AOK must align with the initial outline or the discrepancy needs explicit justification.
Use of first-person (âI will exploreâ) is overly informal for TOK; adopting a more objective academic tone enhances the essayâs formality and persuasiveness.
The counterclaim on dance adaptability (capoeira example) is well chosen to illustrate resilience, showing awareness of an alternative perspective.
This summary of the dance section reiterates previous points without further evaluation; it would be stronger with concise synthesis that ties back to the title question.
The discussion of ballet history is descriptive without analyzing how its knowledge-production methods or WOKs contribute to the perceived fragility of this tradition.
There is no explicitly formulated knowledge question guiding the essay; framing the discussion around a clear KQ would strengthen the TOK approach and focus the analysis.
The claim that dance knowledge is fragile due to culture remains too broad; the student should specify which ways of knowing are at work and how perspective shapes this fragility.
Citations of Anna Kisselgoff and Swan Lake are used as name-drops; the student should engage critically with these sources to show how they support or challenge the argument.
The paragraph on Native American dances ends abruptly, leaving the argument incomplete; the student should finish the thought with clear analysis of how bans reveal knowledge fragility.
The transition into the counterclaim is abrupt; smoother signposting linking back to the title would improve coherence and remind the reader of the central question.
The contrast drawn between adaptability in dance and evolution in physics is a clear synthesis that highlights interdisciplinary insight.
The discussion of capoeira lacks connection to specific ways of knowing such as emotion or memory; explicitly naming WOKs would deepen the TOK analysis.
The Koshare appropriation example is relevant but the student does not consider indigenous perspectives; discussing how source communities view this event would add depth.
Reliance on a blog (Intercontinentalry) weakens academic rigour; incorporating more scholarly or peer-reviewed sources would strengthen evidential support.
The point on misapplication of Newtonian mechanics outside its domain effectively illustrates how context dependence can undermine reliability in science.
The concluding statement on this page ends mid-sentence; a complete and explicit judgement relating back to the title would improve clarity and closure.
The final conclusion restates the main points but lacks deeper evaluation of implications or real-world significance; consider exploring broader consequences of knowledge fragility.
The physics introduction defines robustness in generic terms but does not engage with TOK concepts such as justification or certainty; terminology should be embedded in TOK framework.
Although the counterclaim is relevant, it lacks a link back to the idea of fragility in the prescribed title; ensure counterarguments explicitly address the central question.
An OCR error (âchanicsâ) interrupts the flow; ensure transcription accuracy to maintain formal coherence and reader engagement.
The discussion of the quantum revolution and wave-particle duality provides a strong example of paradigm shifts, clearly illustrating fragility in physics knowledge.
The quantum mechanics example would benefit from an explicit knowledge question (e.g., âTo what extent does changing evidence reshape core theories?â) to anchor the analysis.
The bibliography relies heavily on blogs and popular media; including more peer-reviewed or academic sources would strengthen the credibility of the essayâs evidence base.
Citation entries lack consistent formatting (e.g., missing page numbers, inconsistent italics); adhering strictly to a single citation style enhances professionalism and clarity.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
